Enterprise has no formal impact focus at all; no KPIs, nor impact indicators. The enterprise has to build an IMM strategy and system from scratch. The TA support will focus on raising awareness on the value of IMM (insight in and demonstration of impact) and getting the enterprises acquainted with concepts, tools and frameworks.
Enterprise needs and will benefit from follow-on (bonus) support to operationalise the IMM process (completed during the standard component of the programme).

FlexEAU is looking for various funding sources (of long-term financing) in order to bridge the public funding gap and to compensate for losses due to suspended investment in assets by the government. They are in conversations with various (impact) investors, hence any support will be useful. The enterprise is mainly looking for expansion of solarized boreholes (20 additional). Enterprise has several funding leads, but there is a big gap between realised vs. targeted fundraising. Relevant documents for fundraising (e.g. pitchdeck) are outdated and there is no clear and realistic fundraising strategy.

Impactful business with a talented entrepreneur, yet there was not yet an opportunity to develop an impact model and framework (Theory of Change) with indicators to capture the impact that is created by the enterprise�s business and services. Mobi-Water only has a few general business KPIs. IMM support will provide a structured approach to build an IMM system and get the right tools to collect impact data. This will enable the enterprise to articulate its impact to key stakeholders (potential partners and customers that cater to low-income populations in rural and urban areas) and with that scale impact. Moreover, the enterprise identified customer segmentation to be one of its key barriers. In the process establishing the various elements of an IMM syste (e.g. impact model, customer touchpoint journey) there will be explicit focus on (diversification of) customers segments. This will build on the enterprise�s need and ambition to further seek revenues for more commercial clients (currently 51% / 76% of revenues) in order to subsidise costs for (and with that grow demand from) rural and urban (slum) end customers (currently 49% / 24 % of revenues) with low purchasing power. The combination with the IR track will be beneficial, as the customer segmentation will be linked to the enterprise�s financial model.

Maji Milele is more advanced commercially (compared to average enterprise applying for the programme) and very much impact-focused. The enterprise already achieves quite some impact, yet without being able to systematically demonstrate it. They collect quite a lot of data (but output-focused only) and have a few data points, but do not use it for performance/impact measurement and subsequent decision-making. A solid IMM system will greatly benefit the enterprise to define the enterprise�s overall impact statement/model as well as per business line, structure data and inform decision-making. A structured IMM approach will enable the enterprise to demonstrate impact and enhance their investment proposition.

One of the main barriers perceived by the enterprise is lack of capital to grow their role as service provider, particularly to scale to other counties (within Kenya). When being able to scale/expand geographically, the enterprise can increase impact. There has been modest successful fundraising, but the IR support of the ILF for WASH Foundational Support Programme will greatly benefit the enterprise by means of, among others, support to create basic documents like a proper pitch deck (not available yet), review existing documents (e.g. investor memorandum), review financial model, check the feasibility of investment target, advisory on instruments and terms sheet, etc. Given the enterprise�s search for funders as well as co-investment opportunities, the service provider will also focus on creating a structured investor/funder hitlist + facilitate introductions.

The enterprise aims to commence fundraising in the coming months. They particularly show interest in address the following identified needs: 1) Have a clear understanding of what investors are looking for; 2) How to prepare for the investment process by developing a clear investment strategy; 3) Identify potential investors that are a good match for their business; and 4) identify any gaps or weaknesses in their financial model and plan. The enterprise therefore needs targeted support, expertise and guidance throughout the investment process. This will enable them to be well-prepared for the challenges and opportunities that come with raising venture capital funds (which is the enterprise�s priority).

The enterprise has to build an IMM strategy and system from scratch. The TA support will focus on raising awareness on the value of IMM (insight in and demonstration of impact) and getting the enterprises acquainted with concepts, tools and frameworks.
The enterprise is looking for various funding sources in order to bridge the public funding gap. Any support in relation to investment readiness will be useful

The enterprise participated in an incubation programme of YWS in the past, just got her PoC, and is very early-stage.Even though the enterprise is very early stage, it is short-listed for a recoverable loan from the public sanitation fund of Orbank with GGGI.Further, one of the barriers perceived by the entrepreneur is financial literacy and investment negotiation skills. It is indicated the enterprises has missed investment opportunities due to their lack of ability to deal and negotiate with potential investors.The IR Foundational Support programme could highly benefit ART PESOUNG to explore if that loan opportunity from GGGI is a good opportunity and explore other sources of investment, while refining their business model and financial model.

Fill in the total number of people reached with your initiative at this exact time of measurement (beneficiaries, customers, users etc) with all of the Water, sanitation and IWRM activities. number of users (Aggregate nr of beneficiaries under drinking water, sanitation and IWRM and Capacity building)

Fill in total number of people currently reached with capacity building activities by the initiative, based upon documentation. Disaggregate Male/Female and disaggregate per type of event and provide nr of people per event.

Total nr of individuals that have gained a position as a result of initiative at this time of measurement . Disaggregate by number of new jobs created and number of entrepreneurs/self-employed positions. Disaggregate by male/female. \*\* Minimum requirement to the reporting indicator should follow National standards on daily wages and working conditions (Max 8 hours per day and max 6 days a week)

Total nr of beneficiaries disaggregated by Male/Female and Domestic/school/public users Please also check the criteria per service level to see what level best matches your initiative. The initiative must fulfill all criteria to be considered at that service level. 1. Safely managed service: 2. the initiative provides an improved source of water (this includes: piped water, boreholes or tube wells, protected dug wells, protected springs, rainwater, packaged / bottled water, water that is delivered) 3. the initiative is located on the premises of the household or dwelling 4. the initiative provides drinking water whenever it is needed 5. the drinking water provided by the initiative has no presence of E.coli 6. the drinking water provided by the initiative contains no / limited levels of arsenic and fluoride (below WHO standards: < 0.01 mg/L arsenic and < 1.5mg/L fluoride 7. Basic service: 8. the initiative provides an improved source of water (this includes: piped water, boreholes or tube wells, protected dug wells, protected springs, rainwater, packaged / bottled water, water that is delivered) 9. the initiative provides water for users where the collection time is less than 30 minutes for a roundtrip including queuing 0. Limited service: Drinking water from an improved source for which collection time exceeds 30 minutes for a roundtrip including queuing. 1. Unimproved service: Drinking water from an unprotected dug well or unprotected spring \* This is indicative of the reach and scale of the initiative in reaching new users with its products and services in terms of the service level that is being provided (as defined by JMP).

Fill in Number of individual customers that paid for the initiative's products or services. (AVERAGE PER MONTH) calculated over past 3-12 months. \* Note to enumerator/pm: One customer usually provides one household with a product or service (Average 4/5 persons depending on demographics) Please take this along in analysis.
Select current stage of your innovation/initiative
Provide total number of water facilities per type of facility (Nr of tap points per type) produced and provided by the initiative to users at time of measurement. Choose from list.

Determine the number of separate operational/business units./organizations. This means the revenue generating operations of the program that will remain after program ending or separate business units that are operating under the program. In case of a franchise or connected operation falling under the same initiative you may aggregate the data and fill in one unit only _Calculate Net profit Margin as a Percentage: Net Profit margin = Net Profit ? Total revenue x 100. Net profit is calculated by deducting all company expenses from its total revenue. The result of the profit margin calculation is a percentage � for example, a 10% profit margin means for each $1 of revenue the company earns $0.10 in net profit. Revenue represents the total sales of the company in a period. Calculate over minimum of 6 months._

Fill in total number of unique current users for sanitation. Disaggregated per: Male/Female. Type: Domestic users (private and households), school users and for other public users (e.g markets and hospitals, communal users etc) Finally disaggregate users that are provided with a Safely managed / Basic service / Limited service from the initiative. The initiative must tick all criteria to be considered as that service level. 1) Safely managed service: - the initiative provides an improved sanitation facility (this includes: flush/pour flush to piped sewer system, septic tanks or pit latrines; ventilated improved pit latrines, composting toilets or pit latrines with slabs) - the initiative's sanitation service is not shared with other households - the initiative ensures that human excreta are safely disposed in situ or transported and treated off-site 2) Basic service: - the initiative provides an improved sanitation facility (this includes: flush/pour flush to piped sewer system, septic tanks or pit latrines; ventilated improved pit latrines, composting toilets or pit latrines with slabs) - the initiative's sanitation facility is not shared with other households - initiative does not ensure that human excreta and urine is safely disposed / transported / treated 3) Limited service: Usually at baseline level, but cannot be included at target level. This includes, open pits, unimproved pit latrines, unlined pits, no slabs, prone to flooding etc \* This is indicative of the reach and scale of the initiative in reaching new users with its products and services in terms of the service level that is being provided (as defined by JMP).

Number of litres water used and sold on average per day. (Calculated over a period of at least 1 month and preferably 3 months) Divide total volume by nr of days calculated. E.g if volume is measured, divide monthly volume by nr of days per month.
